raw_consumer_statement:
I submitted a formal dispute to American Express for a {$260.00} charge from XXXX XXXX related to a flight that should not have been billed to me. I provided detailed documentation explaining that the charge was caused by a known system error at XXXX XXXX, which multiple agents acknowledged. I clearly explained that the issue was not whether I made the purchase, but that I should never have been charged in the first place due to a mishandled rebooking after a canceled flight. 

Despite sending in extensive documentation ( including emails, written statements, and agent confirmations ), American Express denied the dispute on the basis that I had " purchased the ticket '' ignoring the actual reason for the dispute. It appears that none of the evidence I submitted was meaningfully reviewed. 

After the dispute was closed, I requested access to the documents I uploaded to the Amex portal ( which took over XXXX hours to prepare ). I was told that I can not retrieve these documents because they are now internal and no longer available to me, even though I am the one who submitted them. I was also told by a representative that Amex has no way to provide them, until I mentioned I would file a CFPB complaint at which point they suddenly opened a document request that may take XXXX days. 

I am now preparing legal action against XXXX XXXX, and I need this documentation to support my case. I am requesting that American Express : XXXX. Provide all documentation I submitted through their dispute portal. 


XXXX. Re-evaluate the handling of my dispute, considering the actual substance of my complaint, not just the fact that a transaction occurred. 

Additionally, I was told by XXXX American Express representative that the company might write off the charge internally and that I would not be rebilled. However, other representatives have told me the opposite that I will be billed again and that it could happen weeks or even months after the dispute closure. I have now spoken with multiple representatives and received completely contradictory information, with no one able to provide a clear or consistent explanation or written confirmation. 

As of now, I have no idea when or if the {$260.00} will be billed back to my card. American Express has created significant financial uncertainty and has offered no formal resolution. This lack of transparency and accountability has left me in an ongoing state of confusion despite my consistent efforts to follow their process. I am now considering legal action against American Express in addition to my action against the merchant.
